label: short
chain_of_thought:
Okay

We are asked to compute:

$$
\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{7} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{1000}{11} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{1000}{13} \right\rfloor
$$

Let's compute each floor value individually.

---

### Step 1: Compute $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{7} \right\rfloor$

$$
\frac{1000}{7} = 142.857...
$$

$$
\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{7} \right\rfloor = 142
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{11} \right\rfloor$

$$
\frac{1000}{11} = 90.909...
$$

$$
\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{11} \right\rfloor = 90
$$

---

### Step 3: Compute $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{13} \right\rfloor$

$$
\frac{1000}{13} = 76.923...
$$

$$
\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{13} \right\rfloor = 76
$$

---

### Step 4: Add the results

$$
142 + 90 + 76 = 308
$$

---

### Ô£à Final Answer:

$$
\boxed{308}
$$
